source_paper_title: Zero-Sum Mean-Field Dynkin Games: Characterization and Convergence
context:
Page 27, Remark 4.1 (end of Section 4, Propagation of chaos)

The question of convergence of \(S\)-saddle points of the particle system to those of the limit process is more elaborate and will be addressed in a forthcoming paper.
title: Convergence of S-saddle points from interacting Dynkin games to the mean-field limit
latex:
Let \((\Omega,\mathcal F,(\mathcal F_t)_{t\in[0,T]},\mathbb P)\) carry independent copies \((B^i,N^i)_{i\ge1}\) of a Brownian motion and a compensated Poisson random measure, and fix \(T>0\). For each \(n\in\mathbb N\), consider the weakly interacting zero-sum Dynkin game of the paper with value process \(V^{i,n}=(V^{i,n}_t)_{t\in[0,T]}\) for player \(i\in\{1,\dots,n\}\), where the payoff of player \(i\) depends on \(V^{i,n}\) and on the empirical measure \(L_n[V^n_t]=\frac1n\sum_{j=1}^n\delta_{V^{j,n}_t}\). Assume the paper’s standing hypotheses ensuring existence of a value and S-saddle points for each particle system (e.g. Assumptions 2.1 and 3.1 and the saddle-point conditions of Theorem 3.2), and assume propagation of chaos holds so that \(V^{i,n}\Rightarrow V^i\) as \(n\to\infty\), where \(V^i\) are i.i.d. copies of the mean-field game value process \(V\).

For a fixed stopping time \(S\in\mathcal T_0\), define the particle-system S-saddle points by
\[
\tau^{\star,i,n}_S := \inf\{t\in[S,T]:\ V^{i,n}_t = h_1(t, V^{i,n}_t, L_n[V^n_t])\},\qquad
\sigma^{\star,i,n}_S := \inf\{t\in[S,T]:\ V^{i,n}_t = h_2(t, V^{i,n}_t, L_n[V^n_t])\},
\]
where \(h_1\) and \(h_2\) are the lower/upper obstacle functions.
Define the mean-field S-saddle points analogously by
\[
\tau^{\star,i}_S := \inf\{t\in[S,T]:\ V^{i}_t = h_1(t, V^{i}_t, \mathcal L(V^{i}_t))\},\qquad
\sigma^{\star,i}_S := \inf\{t\in[S,T]:\ V^{i}_t = h_2(t, V^{i}_t, \mathcal L(V^{i}_t))\},
\]
where \(\mathcal L(V^{i}_t)\) denotes the law of \(V^{i}_t\).

Problem: establish a rigorous convergence result for these saddle-point stopping times as \(n\to\infty\). Concretely, find verifiable sufficient conditions under which, for each fixed \(i\) and \(S\),
\[
(\tau^{\star,i,n}_S,\sigma^{\star,i,n}_S) \Rightarrow (\tau^{\star,i}_S,\sigma^{\star,i}_S) \quad \text{as } n\to\infty,
\]
in an appropriate mode of convergence for stopping times (e.g. convergence in probability, stable convergence, or convergence in law on \([0,T]^2\)), and/or prove convergence of the induced saddle-point payoffs:
\[
\mathbb E\big[ R^{i,n}_S(\tau^{\star,i,n}_S,\sigma^{\star,i,n}_S, V^n) \big] \to \mathbb E\big[ R_S(\tau^{\star,i}_S,\sigma^{\star,i}_S, V) \big].
\]
Here \(R^{i,n}_S\) is the particle payoff functional and \(R_S\) is its mean-field limit, both as defined in the paper.
